summary: Tested Trend Engine v1 integration with Growth Engine. All 10 backend tests passed (100%). Frontend UI verified - Growth button opens GrowthPanel with Trending section at bottom of sidebar showing top 5 trends with source badges (Google/HN). Refresh button fetches new trends. All existing growth endpoints (crawl, analyze, pages, delete) still work correctly.

test_classes: TestTrendsAuth (2 tests) - GET /api/trends and POST /api/trends/fetch return 401 without auth
TestTrendsFetch (1 test) - POST /api/trends/fetch with auth returns {success: true, count: N}
TestTrendsList (1 test) - GET /api/trends with auth returns {trends: [...]} with keyword, source, score, created_at
TestExistingGrowthEndpoints (5 tests) - Crawl, pages list, analyze, page detail with fixes, delete all work
TestTrendIntegrationWithAnalyze (1 test) - Fetch trends then analyze works (trend injection into prompt)

features_tested.feature: Growth button visible in TopBar after login
GrowthPanel has Trending section at bottom of sidebar
Analyze still works and returns opportunities + fixes

seed_data_creation: Fetched 25 trends (10 Google Trends, 15 Hacker News) during testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Trend Engine v1 fully integrated with Growth Engine. Trends are fetched from Google Trends RSS and Hacker News API, stored in MongoDB trend_signals collection. Sidebar shows top 5 trends with source badges. Refresh button calls POST /api/trends/fetch then GET /api/trends. Analyze endpoint injects relevant trends into AI prompt. Reddit was intentionally NOT used (blocks server requests).
